from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Predicate mapping for natural language
    predicate_map = {
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
        "ingredient": lambda s, o: f"contains {o}",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"originates from {o}",
        "division": lambda s, o: f"belongs to division {o}",
        "family": lambda s, o: f"family {o}",
        "order": lambda s, o: f"order {o}",
        "course": lambda s, o: f"served as a {o}",
        "dishVariation": lambda s, o: f"variations include {o}",
        "mainIngredient": lambda s, o: f"main ingredients are {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"led by {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"also known as {o}",
        "creatorOfDish": lambda s, o: f"created by {o}",
        "creator": lambda s, o: f"created by {o}",
        "servingTemperature": lambda s, o: f"served {o}",
        "served": lambda s, o: f"served {o}",
        "carbohydrate": lambda s, o: f"{o} carbohydrates",
        "fat": lambda s, o: f"{o} fat",
        "protein": lambda s, o: f"{o} protein",
        "year": lambda s, o: f"introduced in {o}",
        "related": lambda s, o: f"related to {o}",
        "similarDish": lambda s, o: f"similar to {o}",
        "genus": lambda s, o: f"genus {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"language {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"part of {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"currency {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people are {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"capital is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"ethnic group {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"founded in {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"official language {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"leader title {o}",
        "servingSize": lambda s, o: f"serving size {o}",
        "class": lambda s, o: f"a {o}",
    }

    # Priority for ordering predicates
    food_predicates = {"mainIngredient", "ingredient", "dishVariation", "course", "servingTemperature", "served", "similarDish", "related"}
    geo_predicates = {"country", "region", "isPartOf", "capital", "leader", "currency", "demonym", "ethnicGroup", "language", "officialLanguage"}
    class_predicates = {"division", "family", "order", "genus", "class"}
    other_predicates = {"creator", "creatorOfDish", "alternativeName", "year", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein", "servingSize", "foundationPlace", "leaderTitle"}

    def get_priority(p):
        if p in food_predicates:
            return 0
        elif p in geo_predicates:
            return 1
        elif p in class_predicates:
            return 2
        return 3

    # Build sentence from main subject first
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_triples = subject_triples.get(main_subject, [])
    main_triples.sort(key=lambda t: get_priority(t.predicate))

    # Build main sentence
    sentence_parts = []
    first = True
    for t in main_triples:
        if t.predicate in predicate_map:
            if first:
                sentence_parts.append(predicate_map[t.predicate](t.subject, t.object))
                first = False
            else:
                sentence_parts.append(predicate_map[t.predicate](t.subject, t.object))

    # Add related subjects (like country, region info)
    for subject, tlist in subject_triples.items():
        if subject != main_subject:
            for t in tlist:
                if t.predicate in predicate_map:
                    sentence_parts.append(predicate_map[t.predicate](t.subject, t.object))

    if not sentence_parts:
        return ""

    # Combine into one coherent sentence
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        return sentence_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        return ", ".join(sentence_parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject for better sentence construction
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Priority order for predicates to create coherent sentences
    food_predicates = ["mainIngredient", "ingredient", "dishVariation", "course", "servingTemperature", "served", "similarDish", "related"]
    geographic_predicates = ["country", "region", "isPartOf", "capital", "leader", "currency", "demonym", "ethnicGroup", "language", "officialLanguage"]
    classification_predicates = ["division", "family", "order", "genus", "class"]
    other_predicates = ["creator", "creatorOfDish", "alternativeName", "year", "carbohydrate", "fat", "protein", "servingSize", "foundationPlace", "leaderTitle"]

    def get_predicate_priority(p):
        if p in food_predicates:
            return 0
        elif p in geographic_predicates:
            return 1
        elif p in classification_predicates:
            return 2
        else:
            return 3

    def predicate_to_phrase(s, p, o):
        """Convert a triple to a natural language phrase"""
        mapping = {
            "country": f"{s} is from {o}",
            "ingredient": f"{s} contains {o}",
            "region": f"{s} originates from {o}",
            "division": f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
            "family": f"{s} is in the family {o}",
            "order": f"{s} is classified under the order {o}",
            "course": f"{s} is served as a {o}",
            "dishVariation": f"{s} has variations such as {o}",
            "mainIngredient": f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}",
            "leader": f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
            "alternativeName": f"{s} is also known as {o}",
            "creatorOfDish": f"{s} was created by {o}",
            "creator": f"{s} was created by {o}",
            "servingTemperature": f"{s} is served {o}",
            "served": f"{s} is served {o}",
            "carbohydrate": f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates",
            "fat": f"{s} contains {o} of fat",
            "protein": f"{s} contains {o} of protein",
            "year": f"{s} was introduced in {o}",
            "related": f"{s} is related to {o}",
            "similarDish": f"{s} is similar to {o}",
            "genus": f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}",
            "language": f"The language of {s} is {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"{s} is part of {o}",
            "currency": f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
            "demonym": f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
            "capital": f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
            "ethnicGroup": f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
            "foundationPlace": f"{s} was founded in {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
            "servingSize": f"The serving size of {s} is {o}",
            "class": f"{s} is a {o}",
        }
        return mapping.get(p, f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Build sentence parts for each subject
    sentence_parts = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_triples.items():
        # Sort predicates by priority for better flow
        sorted_triples = sorted(tlist, key=lambda t: get_predicate_priority(t.predicate))

        parts = [predicate_to_phrase(t.subject, t.predicate, t.object) for t in sorted_triples]
        sentence_parts.append(parts)

    # Combine all parts into a coherent sentence
    all_parts = []
    for parts in sentence_parts:
        all_parts.extend(parts)

    if not all_parts:
        return ""

    # Build a more natural sentence structure
    if len(all_parts) == 1:
        return all_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        # Connect related parts with appropriate conjunctions
        result_parts = []
        for i, part in enumerate(all_parts):
            if i == 0:
                result_parts.append(part)
            elif i == len(all_parts) - 1:
                result_parts.append("and " + part)
            else:
                result_parts.append(part)

        return ", ".join(result_parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
